Analyzed URL Structure Components\n\n**Date Encoding (YYYYMMDD):**\n- All NOAA models use consistent 8-digit format\n- YYYY = 4-digit year, MM = 2-digit month, DD = 2-digit day\n- Example: `20260703` = July 3, 2026\n\n**Cycle Hour Encoding:**\n- Three patterns identified:\n  - `HH` (two-digit hour): NAM directory naming\n  - `CC` (two-digit hour): RAP, RRFS file naming\n  - `tCCz` (\"t\" + hour + \"z\"): HRRR, NAM, RAP file naming\n\n**Forecast Hour Encoding:**\n- Three patterns identified:\n  - `fFF` (\"f\" + two-digit): HRRR, RAP (f00-f48)\n  - `FF` (two-digit, no prefix): NAM (00-60)\n  - `FFF` (three-digit): RRFS, NBM (000-084+)\n\n### 2. Documented Model-Specific Patterns\n\nDocumented 8 NOAA models with URL patterns and examples:\n\n| Model | Pattern | Cycle Times | Forecast Range |\n|-------|---------|-------------|----------------|\n| HRRR | `hrrr.YYYYMMDD/conus/hrrr.tCCz.wrfsfcfFF.grib2` | Hourly (00z-23z) | f00-f48 |\n| NAM | `nam.YYYYMMDD/nam.tCCz.[product]FF.tm00.grib2` | 6-hourly (00,06,12,18z) | 00-60 |\n| RAP | `YYYYMMDD/HH/rap.tCCz.awp130pgrbfFF.grib2` | Hourly (00z-23z) | f00-f51 |\n| RRFS | `YYYYMMDD/HH/rrfs.tCCz.[product].fFF.grib2` | Hourly (00z-23z) | f000-f084 |\n| NBM | `blend.YYYYMMDD/HH/core/blend.tCCz.core.fFFF.co.grib2` | 6-hourly (00,06,12,18z) | f001-f084 |\n| HREF | `href.YYYYMMDD/HH/` (NOMADS) | ~4x daily | Varies by member |\n| SREF | `sref.YYYYMMDD/HH/` (NOMADS) | 4x daily (03,09,15,21z) | Varies by member |\n| RTMA | `YYYYMMDD/HH/rtma.tCCz.2dvaranl.[domain].grib2` | Hourly (00z-23z) | Analysis only |\n\n### 3. Provided Step-by-Step Construction Examples\n\nCreated 3 detailed examples showing how to construct URLs from scratch:\n- HRRR: December 25, 2025, 18z cycle, 6-hour forecast\n- NAM: March 15, 2026, 00z cycle, analysis\n- NBM: September 10, 2026, 12z cycle, 24-hour forecast\n\n### 4. Documented Common Errors\n\nIdentified 4 common URL construction errors:\n1. Missing leading zeros (dates/hours)\n2. Incorrect forecast hour format (2-digit vs 3-digit)\n3. Wrong product suffix\n4. Non-existent cycle times\n\n### 5. Platform Comparison\n\nDocumented three archive platforms:\n- AWS S3: 6 models (HRRR, NAM, RAP, RRFS, RTMA, NBM)\n- NOMADS: 4 models (HREF, SREF, NAM, RAP, HRRR alternative)\n- FTP: 2 models (NDFD, WAVEWATCH3)\n\n## Acceptance Criteria Met\n\n✅ **For each model, documented the URL pattern with placeholders**\n- All 8 models documented with clear pattern definitions\n- Placeholders (YYYYMMDD, CC, FF, FFF, etc.) clearly explained\n\n✅ **Provided 2-3 example URLs per model**\n- HRRR: 4 examples\n- NAM: 3 examples\n- RAP: 3 examples\n- RRFS: 3 examples\n- NBM: 3 examples\n- HREF: 2 examples\n- SREF: 2 examples\n- RTMA: 3 examples\n\n✅ **Noted variations in URL structure**\n- Forecast hour encoding variations (f00 vs 00 vs f000 vs f001)\n- Cycle frequency variations (hourly vs 6-hourly vs 4x daily)\n- Platform variations (AWS vs NOMADS vs FTP)\n- Product type variations documented\n\n## Files Created\n\n1. `/home/coding/gribtract/docs/research/bf-5gsm-noaa-url-patterns.md` - Comprehensive URL pattern documentation (17,256 bytes)\n2. `/home/coding/gribtract/notes/bf-5gsm.md` - This summary\n\n## Related Documentation\n\n- `docs/research/bf-1ot3-gdt330-candidate-archive-urls.md` - Archive URLs for GDT 3.30 candidates\n- `docs/research/noaa-archive-urls.md` - Primary model archive URLs\n\n## Date Completed\n\n2026-07-03\n","numLines":100,"startLine":1,"totalLines":100}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_6d86ddc3c0e44a26a35009bc","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# NOAA Archive URL Patterns: Dates, Cycles, and Forecast Hours\n2\t\n3\t## Summary\n4\t\n5\tThis document provides a comprehensive reference for constructing NOAA GRIB2 archive URLs by explaining how dates, cycle runs, and forecast hours are encoded in the URL structure for different models.\n6\t\n7\t**Date:** 2026-07-03  \n8\t**Purpose:** Reference for constructing URLs to access NOAA model data archives\n9\t\n10\t---\n11\t\n12\t## URL Pattern Components\n13\t\n14\t### 1. Date Encoding (YYYYMMDD)\n15\t\n16\tAll NOAA models use the **YYYYMMDD** format for dates in URLs:\n17\t- **YYYY** = 4-digit year (e.g., 2026)\n18\t- **MM** = 2-digit month (01-12)\n19\t- **DD** = 2-digit day (01-31)\n20\t\n21\t**Examples:**\n22\t- `20260703` = July 3, 2026\n23\t- `20240601` = June 1, 2024\n24\t- `20250115` = January 15, 2025\n25\t\n26\t### 2. Cycle Hour Encoding (HH, CC, or tCCz)\n27\t\n28\tCycle hours indicate when the model run was initiated. Encoding varies by model:\n29\t\n30\t| Pattern | Description | Usage |\n31\t|---------|-------------|-------|\n32\t| **HH** | Two-digit hour (00-23) | NAM directory naming |\n33\t| **CC** | Two-digit hour (00-23) | RAP, RRFS file naming |\n34\t| **tCCz** | \"t\" + two-digit hour + \"z\" | HRRR, NAM, RAP file naming |\n35\t\n36\t**Standard Cycle Times by Model:**\n37\t- **HRRR:** Hourly (00z, 01z, 02z, ..., 23z)\n38\t- **RAP:** Hourly (00z, 01z, 02z, ..., 23z)\n39\t- **RRFS:** Hourly (00z, 01z, 02z, ..., 23z)\n40\t- **NAM:** 6-hourly (00z, 06z, 12z, 18z)\n41\t- **NBM:** 6-hourly (00z, 06z, 12z, 18z)\n42\t- **HREF:** Typically (00z, 06z, 12z, 18z)\n43\t- **SREF:** 4x daily (03z, 09z, 15z, 21z)\n44\t\n45\t### 3. Forecast Hour Encoding (fFF, FF, or FFF)\n46\t\n47\tForecast hours indicate how far into the future the prediction extends. Encoding varies:\n48\t\n49\t| Pattern | Description | Range | Usage |\n50\t|---------|-------------|-------|-------|\n51\t| **fFF** | \"f\" + two-digit hour | f00-f48 | HRRR directory naming |\n52\t| **FF** | Two-digit hour | 00-60+ | NAM, RAP file suffix |\n53\t| **FFF** | Three-digit hour | 001-084+ | NBM file suffix |\n54\t\n55\t**Forecast Hour Examples:**\n56\t- `f00` or `00` = Analysis (initial time step)\n57\t- `f03` or `03` = 3-hour forecast\n58\t- `f12` or `12` = 12-hour forecast\n59\t- `f48` or `48` = 48-hour forecast\n60\t\n61\t---\n62\t\n63\t## Model-Specific URL Patterns\n64\t\n65\t### 1. HRRR (High-Resolution Rapid Refresh)\n66\t\n67\t**URL Pattern:**\n68\t```\n69\thttps://noaa-hrrr-bdp-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/hrrr.YYYYMMDD/conus/hrrr.tCCz.wrfsfcfFF.grib2\n70\t```\n71\t\n72\t**Components:**\n73\t- **YYYYMMDD** = Model run date (e.g., `20260703`)\n74\t- **CC** = Cycle hour (00-23)\n75\t- **FF** = Forecast hour (00-48)\n76\t\n77\t**Example URLs:**\n78\t```\n79\t# July 3, 2026, 00z cycle, analysis (f00)\n80\thttps://noaa-hrrr-bdp-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/hrrr.20260703/conus/hrrr.t00z.wrfsfcf00.grib2\n81\t\n82\t# July 3, 2026, 06z cycle, 3-hour forecast (f03)\n83\thttps://noaa-hrrr-bdp-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/hrrr.20260703/conus/hrrr.t06z.wrfsfcf03.grib2\n84\t\n85\t# July 3, 2026, 12z cycle, 12-hour forecast (f12)\n86\thttps://noaa-hrrr-bdp-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/hrrr.20260703/conus/hrrr.t12z.wrfsfcf12.grib2\n87\t\n88\t# June 1, 2024, 12z cycle, analysis (f00)\n89\thttps://noaa-hrrr-bdp-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/hrrr.20240601/conus/hrrr.t12z.wrfsfcf00.grib2\n90\t```\n91\t\n92\t**Alternative AWS Structure (with subdirectories):**\n93\t```\n94\thttps://noaa-hrrr-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/YYYYMMDD/HH/fFF/hrrr.tCCz.wrfsfcfFF.grib2\n95\t```\n96\t- **HH** = Cycle hour directory (00-23)\n97\t- **fFF** = Forecast hour directory (f00-f48)\n98\t\n99\t**Examples with subdirectories:**\n100\t```\n101\thttps://noaa-hrrr-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/20260703/00/f00/hrrr.t00z.wrfsfcf00.grib2\n102\thttps://noaa-hrrr-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/20260703/06/f12/hrrr.t06z.wrfsfcf12.grib2\n103\t```\n104\t\n105\t**Key Variations:**\n106\t- **Product types:** `wrfsfc` (surface), `wrfprs` (pressure), `wrfnat` (native)\n107\t- **Sub-hourly:** `wrfsubhfFF.MM.grib2` (MM = minute offset)\n108\t\n109\t---\n110\t\n111\t### 2. NAM CONUS (North American Mesoscale)\n112\t\n113\t**URL Pattern:**\n114\t```\n115\thttps://noaa-nam-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/nam.YYYYMMDD/nam.tCCz.[product]FF.tm00.grib2\n116\t```\n117\t\n118\t**Components:**\n119\t- **YYYYMMDD** = Model run date (e.g., `20260703`)\n120\t- **CC** = Cycle hour (00, 06, 12, 18 only)\n121\t- **[product]** = Product type (awphys, conusnest, etc.)\n122\t- **FF** = Forecast hour (00-60)\n123\t\n124\t**Example URLs:**\n125\t```\n126\t# July 2, 2026, 12z cycle, analysis (00)\n127\thttps://noaa-nam-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/nam.20260702/nam.t12z.awphys00.tm00.grib2\n128\t\n129\t# July 2, 2026, 12z cycle, 12-hour forecast (12)\n130\thttps://noaa-nam-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/nam.20260702/nam.t12z.awphys12.tm00.grib2\n131\t\n132\t# January 15, 2025, 00z cycle, analysis (00)\n133\thttps://noaa-nam-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/nam.20250115/nam.t00z.awphys00.tm00.grib2\n134\t```\n135\t\n136\t**Alternative Directory Structure:**\n137\t```\n138\thttps://noaa-nam-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/YYYYMMDD_HH/\n139\t```\n140\t- **YYYYMMDD_HH** = Date with underscore-separated hour (e.g., `20260703_00`)\n141\t\n142\t**Examples:**\n143\t```\n144\thttps://noaa-nam-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/20260703_00/nam.t00z.awphys00.tm00.grib2\n145\thttps://noaa-nam-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/20260703_12/nam.t12z.awphys12.tm00.grib2\n146\t```\n147\t\n148\t**Key Variations:**\n149\t- **Product types:** `awphys` (AWIP physics grid 218), `conusnest` (CONUS nest)\n150\t- **Cycle limitation:** Only 4 cycles per day (00z, 06z, 12z, 18z)\n151\t\n152\t---\n153\t\n154\t### 3. RAP (Rapid Refresh)\n155\t\n156\t**URL Pattern:**\n157\t```\n158\thttps://noaa-rap-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/YYYYMMDD/HH/rap.tCCz.awp130pgrbfFF.grib2\n159\t```\n160\t\n161\t**Components:**\n162\t- **YYYYMMDD** = Model run date (e.g., `20260703`)\n163\t- **HH** = Cycle hour directory (00-23)\n164\t- **CC** = Cycle hour in filename (00-23)\n165\t- **FF** = Forecast hour (00-51)\n166\t\n167\t**Example URLs:**\n168\t```\n169\t# July 3, 2026, 00z cycle, analysis (f00)\n170\thttps://noaa-rap-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/20260703/00/rap.t00z.awp130pgrbf00.grib2\n171\t\n172\t# July 3, 2026, 12z cycle, 12-hour forecast (f12)\n173\thttps://noaa-rap-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/20260703/12/rap.t12z.awp130pgrbf12.grib2\n174\t\n175\t# July 3, 2026, 06z cycle, 21-hour forecast (f21)\n176\thttps://noaa-rap-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/20260703/06/rap.t06z.awp130pgrbf21.grib2\n177\t```\n178\t\n179\t**Key Variations:**\n180\t- **Grid resolutions:** `awp130pgrb` (13km), `awp252pgrb` (20km), `awp236pgrb` (40km)\n181\t- **Extended forecasts:** 0-51h available at 03z, 09z, 15z, 21z\n182\t- **Standard forecasts:** 0-21h for all cycles\n183\t\n184\t---\n185\t\n186\t### 4. RRFS (Rapid Refresh Forecast System)\n187\t\n188\t**URL Pattern:**\n189\t```\n190\thttps://noaa-rrfs-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/YYYYMMDD/HH/rrfs.tCCz.[product].fFF.grib2\n191\t```\n192\t\n193\t**Components:**\n194\t- **YYYYMMDD** = Model run date (e.g., `20260703`)\n195\t- **HH** = Cycle hour directory (00-23)\n196\t- **CC** = Cycle hour in filename (00-23)\n197\t- **FF** = Forecast hour (000-084+)\n198\t\n199\t**Example URLs:**\n200\t```\n201\t# July 3, 2026, 00z cycle, analysis (f000)\n202\thttps://noaa-rrfs-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/20260703/00/rrfs.t00z.prslev.f000.grib2\n203\t\n204\t# July 3, 2026, 06z cycle, 12-hour forecast (f012)\n205\thttps://noaa-rrfs-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/20260703/06/rrfs.t06z.prslev.f012.grib2\n206\t\n207\t# July 3, 2026, 12z cycle, 48-hour forecast (f048)\n208\thttps://noaa-rrfs-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/20260703/12/rrfs.t12z.prslev.f048.grib2\n209\t```\n210\t\n211\t**Key Variations:**\n212\t- **Product types:** `prslev` (pressure levels), `sfc` (surface), `nat` (native)\n213\t- **Status:** BETA/Prototype (operational ~August 2026)\n214\t- **Forecast range:** 0-18h hourly cycles; 0-84h for 00,06,12,18z cycles\n215\t\n216\t---\n217\t\n218\t### 5. NBM (National Blend of Models)\n219\t\n220\t**URL Pattern:**\n221\t```\n222\thttps://noaa-nbm-grib2-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/blend.YYYYMMDD/HH/core/blend.tCCz.core.fFFF.co.grib2\n223\t```\n224\t\n225\t**Components:**\n226\t- **YYYYMMDD** = Model run date (e.g., `20260703`)\n227\t- **HH** = Cycle hour directory (00, 06, 12, 18)\n228\t- **CC** = Cycle hour in filename (00, 06, 12, 18)\n229\t- **FFF** = Three-digit forecast hour (001-084+)\n230\t\n231\t**Example URLs:**\n232\t```\n233\t# July 3, 2026, 00z cycle, 1-hour forecast (f001)\n234\thttps://noaa-nbm-grib2-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/blend.20260703/00/core/blend.t00z.core.f001.co.grib2\n235\t\n236\t# July 3, 2026, 12z cycle, 12-hour forecast (f012)\n237\thttps://noaa-nbm-grib2-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/blend.20260703/12/core/blend.t12z.core.f012.co.grib2\n238\t\n239\t# July 3, 2026, 18z cycle, 48-hour forecast (f048)\n240\thttps://noaa-nbm-grib2-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/blend.20260703/18/core/blend.t18z.core.f048.co.grib2\n241\t```\n242\t\n243\t**Key Variations:**\n244\t- **Forecast hour encoding:** Always three digits (001, 002, ..., 012, ..., 084)\n245\t- **Cycle limitation:** Only 4 cycles per day (00z, 06z, 12z, 18z)\n246\t- **Product type:** `core` (standard blend product)\n247\t\n248\t---\n249\t\n250\t### 6. HREF (High-Resolution Ensemble Forecast)\n251\t\n252\t**URL Pattern (NOMADS):**\n253\t```\n254\thttps://nomads.ncep.noaa.gov/pub/data/nccf/com/href/prod/href.YYYYMMDD/HH/\n255\t```\n256\t\n257\t**Components:**\n258\t- **YYYYMMDD** = Model run date (e.g., `20260703`)\n259\t- **HH** = Cycle hour directory (00, 06, 12, 18)\n260\t\n261\t**Example URLs:**\n262\t```\n263\t# July 3, 2026, 00z cycle directory\n264\thttps://nomads.ncep.noaa.gov/pub/data/nccf/com/href/prod/href.20260703/00/\n265\t\n266\t# July 3, 2026, 12z cycle directory\n267\thttps://nomads.ncep.noaa.gov/pub/data/nccf/com/href/prod/href.20260703/12/\n268\t\n269\t# Individual ensemble member files would be in these directories\n270\t```\n271\t\n272\t**Key Variations:**\n273\t- **Access method:** NOMADS (directory browsing via HTTPS)\n274\t- **Cycle times:** Typically (00z, 06z, 12z, 18z)\n275\t- **File naming:** Varies by ensemble member (arw, nmm, nssl, etc.)\n276\t\n277\t---\n278\t\n279\t### 7. SREF (Short Range Ensemble Forecast)\n280\t\n281\t**URL Pattern (NOMADS):**\n282\t```\n283\thttps://nomads.ncep.noaa.gov/pub/data/nccf/com/sref/prod/sref.YYYYMMDD/HH/\n284\t```\n285\t\n286\t**Components:**\n287\t- **YYYYMMDD** = Model run date (e.g., `20260703`)\n288\t- **HH** = Cycle hour directory (03, 09, 15, 21)\n289\t\n290\t**Example URLs:**\n291\t```\n292\t# July 3, 2026, 03z cycle directory\n293\thttps://nomads.ncep.noaa.gov/pub/data/nccf/com/sref/prod/sref.20260703/03/\n294\t\n295\t# July 3, 2026, 15z cycle directory\n296\thttps://nomads.ncep.noaa.gov/pub/data/nccf/com/sref/prod/sref.20260703/15/\n297\t```\n298\t\n299\t**Key Variations:**\n300\t- **Cycle times:** Non-standard (03z, 09z, 15z, 21z - 4x daily)\n301\t- **Status:** Proposed for termination July 2025 (may be replaced by GEFS)\n302\t- **File naming:** Includes ensemble member identifiers (ctl, g01, g02, etc.)\n303\t\n304\t---\n305\t\n306\t### 8. RTMA (Real-Time Mesoscale Analysis)\n307\t\n308\t**URL Pattern:**\n309\t```\n310\thttps://noaa-rtma-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/YYYYMMDD/HH/rtma.tCCz.2dvaranl.[domain].grib2\n311\t```\n312\t\n313\t**Components:**\n314\t- **YYYYMMDD** = Analysis date (e.g., `20260703`)\n315\t- **HH** = Analysis hour directory (00-23)\n316\t- **CC** = Analysis hour in filename (00-23)\n317\t- **[domain]** = Domain identifier (conus, ak, hi, pr)\n318\t\n319\t**Example URLs:**\n320\t```\n321\t# July 3, 2026, 00z analysis, CONUS domain\n322\thttps://noaa-rtma-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/20260703/00/rtma.t00z.2dvaranl.conus.grib2\n323\t\n324\t# July 3, 2026, 12z analysis, CONUS domain\n325\thttps://noaa-rtma-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/20260703/12/rtma.t12z.2dvaranl.conus.grib2\n326\t\n327\t# July 3, 2026, 06z analysis, Alaska domain\n328\thttps://noaa-rtma-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/20260703/06/rtma.t06z.2dvaranl.ak.grib2\n329\t```\n330\t\n331\t**Key Variations:**\n332\t- **Product type:** `2dvaranl` (2D variational analysis)\n333\t- **Domains:** `conus`, `ak` (Alaska), `hi` (Hawaii), `pr` (Puerto Rico)\n334\t- **Update frequency:** Hourly analyses\n335\t\n336\t---\n337\t\n338\t## Platform-Specific Pattern Summary\n339\t\n340\t### AWS S3 Pattern (Most Common)\n341\t```\n342\thttps://noaa-[model]-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/[model].YYYYMMDD/[directory]/[file]\n343\t```\n344\tUsed by: HRRR, NAM, RAP, RRFS, RTMA, NBM\n345\t\n346\t**Characteristics:**\n347\t- **Date format:** YYYYMMDD (8 digits, no separators)\n348\t- **Cycle encoding:** Varies (tCCz, CC, or HH directory)\n349\t- **Forecast encoding:** Varies (fFF, FF, or FFF)\n350\t\n351\t### NOMADS Pattern\n352\t```\n353\thttps://nomads.ncep.noaa.gov/pub/data/nccf/com/[model]/prod/[model].YYYYMMDD/HH/\n354\t```\n355\tUsed by: HREF, SREF, NAM, RAP, HRRR (alternative)\n356\t\n357\t**Characteristics:**\n358\t- **Date format:** YYYYMMDD (8 digits, no separators)\n359\t- **Cycle encoding:** HH directory (2-digit hour)\n360\t- **Access method:** Directory browsing via HTTPS\n361\t\n362\t### NOAA FTP Pattern\n363\t```\n364\tftp://[server].noaa.gov/[path]/[model]/YYYYMMDD/[files]\n365\t```\n366\tUsed by: NDFD, WAVEWATCH3\n367\t\n368\t**Characteristics:**\n369\t- **Protocol:** FTP or HTTPS\n370\t- **Path components:** Complex multi-level path with station/format/content identifiers\n371\t- **Date format:** Varies by product\n372\t\n373\t---\n374\t\n375\t## Forecast Hour Encoding Comparison\n376\t\n377\t| Model | Analysis | 1-hour | 3-hour | 12-hour | 48-hour | Range |\n378\t|-------|----------|--------|--------|---------|---------|-------|\n379\t| **HRRR** | f00 | f01 | f03 | f12 | f48 | 00-48 |\n380\t| **NAM** | 00 | 01 | 03 | 12 | 48 | 00-60 |\n381\t| **RAP** | f00 | f01 | f03 | f12 | f48 | 00-51 |\n382\t| **RRFS** | f000 | f001 | f003 | f012 | f048 | 000-084 |\n383\t| **NBM** | f001 | f001 | f003 | f012 | f048 | 001-084 |\n384\t\n385\t**Key Pattern Differences:**\n386\t- **HRRR/NAM/RAP:** Two-digit forecast hours (00, 01, 03, 12, 48)\n387\t- **RRFS/NBM:** Three-digit forecast hours (000, 001, 003, 012, 048)\n388\t- **HRRR:** Uses \"f\" prefix (f00)\n389\t- **NAM:** No prefix (00)\n390\t- **RRFS:** Uses \"f\" prefix with three digits (f000)\n391\t- **NBM:** Uses \"f\" prefix with three digits (f001 - analysis is f001, not f000)\n392\t\n393\t---\n394\t\n395\t## Cycle Frequency Comparison\n396\t\n397\t| Model | Cycles Per Day | Cycle Hours | Format in URL |\n398\t|-------|----------------|-------------|---------------|\n399\t| **HRRR** | 24 | 00z-23z | tCCz (e.g., t00z, t06z, t12z) |\n400\t| **RAP** | 24 | 00z-23z | tCCz (e.g., t00z, t06z, t12z) |\n401\t| **RRFS** | 24 | 00z-23z | tCCz (e.g., t00z, t06z, t12z) |\n402\t| **NAM** | 4 | 00z, 06z, 12z, 18z | tCCz (e.g., t00z, t06z) |\n403\t| **NBM** | 4 | 00z, 06z, 12z, 18z | tCCz (e.g., t00z, t06z) |\n404\t| **HREF** | ~4 | 00z, 06z, 12z, 18z | HH directory |\n405\t| **SREF** | 4 | 03z, 09z, 15z, 21z | HH directory |\n406\t| **RTMA** | 24 | 00z-23z | tCCz (e.g., t00z) |\n407\t\n408\t**Key Pattern Differences:**\n409\t- **Hourly models (HRRR, RAP, RRFS, RTMA):** All 24 cycles available\n410\t- **6-hourly models (NAM, NBM, HREF):** Only 00z, 06z, 12z, 18z\n411\t- **SREF exception:** Uses offset cycle times (03z, 09z, 15z, 21z)\n412\t\n413\t---\n414\t\n415\t## Constructing URLs: Step-by-Step Examples\n416\t\n417\t### Example 1: HRRR - December 25, 2025, 18z cycle, 6-hour forecast\n418\t\n419\t**Step 1:** Encode the date\n420\t- Date: December 25, 2025\n421\t- Encoded: `20251225`\n422\t\n423\t**Step 2:** Encode the cycle hour\n424\t- Cycle: 18z\n425\t- Format: `tCCz` → `t18z`\n426\t\n427\t**Step 3:** Encode the forecast hour\n428\t- Forecast: 6 hours\n429\t- Format: `fFF` → `f06`\n430\t\n431\t**Step 4:** Assemble the URL\n432\t```\n433\thttps://noaa-hrrr-bdp-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/hrrr.20251225/conus/hrrr.t18z.wrfsfcf06.grib2\n434\t```\n435\t\n436\t### Example 2: NAM - March 15, 2026, 00z cycle, analysis\n437\t\n438\t**Step 1:** Encode the date\n439\t- Date: March 15, 2026\n440\t- Encoded: `20260315`\n441\t\n442\t**Step 2:** Encode the cycle hour\n443\t- Cycle: 00z\n444\t- Format: `tCCz` → `t00z`\n445\t\n446\t**Step 3:** Encode the forecast hour\n447\t- Forecast: Analysis (0 hours)\n448\t- Format: `FF` → `00`\n449\t\n450\t**Step 4:** Assemble the URL\n451\t```\n452\thttps://noaa-nam-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/nam.20260315/nam.t00z.awphys00.tm00.grib2\n453\t```\n454\t\n455\t### Example 3: NBM - September 10, 2026, 12z cycle, 24-hour forecast\n456\t\n457\t**Step 1:** Encode the date\n458\t- Date: September 10, 2026\n459\t- Encoded: `20260910`\n460\t\n461\t**Step 2:** Encode the cycle hour\n462\t- Cycle: 12z\n463\t- Format: `tCCz` → `t12z`\n464\t\n465\t**Step 3:** Encode the forecast hour\n466\t- Forecast: 24 hours\n467\t- Format: `fFFF` → `f024` (three digits)\n468\t\n469\t**Step 4:** Assemble the URL\n470\t```\n471\thttps://noaa-nbm-grib2-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/blend.20260910/12/core/blend.t12z.core.f024.co.grib2\n472\t```\n473\t\n474\t---\n475\t\n476\t## Common URL Construction Errors to Avoid\n477\t\n478\t### Error 1: Missing Leading Zeros\n479\t❌ `hrrr.202673/t1z.wrfsfcf3.grib2`  \n480\t✅ `hrrr.20260703/t01z.wrfsfcf03.grib2`\n481\t\n482\t**Explanation:** Dates and hours must always be two digits (with leading zeros).\n483\t\n484\t### Error 2: Incorrect Forecast Hour Format\n485\t❌ `blend.t12z.core.f24.co.grib2`  \n486\t✅ `blend.t12z.core.f024.co.grib2`\n487\t\n488\t**Explanation:** NBM requires three-digit forecast hours (f001, f002, ..., f024).\n489\t\n490\t### Error 3: Wrong Product Suffix\n491\t❌ `hrrr.t12z.wrfprsf00.grib2` (for surface data)  \n492\t✅ `hrrr.t12z.wrfsfcf00.grib2`\n493\t\n494\t**Explanation:** Product suffixes matter - `wrfsfc` for surface, `wrfprs` for pressure.\n495\t\n496\t### Error 4: Non-Existent Cycle\n497\t❌ `nam.20260703/nam.t03z.awphys00.tm00.grib2`  \n498\t✅ `nam.20260703/nam.t00z.awphys00.tm00.grib2` or `nam.t06z`\n499\t\n500\t**Explanation:** NAM only runs 4 cycles per day (00z, 06z, 12z, 18z), not hourly.\n501\t\n502\t---\n503\t\n504\t## Quick Reference Table\n505\t\n506\t| Model | Date Format | Cycle Format | Forecast Format | Example |\n507\t|-------|-------------|--------------|------------------|---------|\n508\t| **HRRR** | YYYYMMDD | tCCz | fFF | hrrr.t12z.wrfsfcf00.grib2 |\n509\t| **NAM** | YYYYMMDD | tCCz | FF | nam.t12z.awphys00.tm00.grib2 |\n510\t| **RAP** | YYYYMMDD | tCCz | fFF | rap.t12z.awp130pgrbf00.grib2 |\n511\t| **RRFS** | YYYYMMDD | tCCz | fFF | rrfs.t12z.prslev.f000.grib2 |\n512\t| **NBM** | YYYYMMDD | tCCz | fFFF | blend.t12z.core.f001.co.grib2 |\n513\t| **RTMA** | YYYYMMDD | tCCz | N/A (analysis) | rtma.t12z.2dvaranl.conus.grib2 |\n514\t| **HREF** | YYYYMMDD | HH dir | Varies | href.20260703/12/ |\n515\t| **SREF** | YYYYMMDD | HH dir | Varies | sref.20260703/09/ |\n516\t\n517\t---\n518\t\n519\t## Acceptance Criteria Verification\n520\t\n521\t✅ **For each model, document the URL pattern with placeholders for date, cycle, and forecast hour**\n522\t- All 8 models documented with clear patterns\n523\t- Placeholders (YYYYMMDD, CC, FF, etc.) clearly defined\n524\t\n525\t✅ **Provide at least 2-3 example URLs with specific dates/cycles/forecast hours**\n526\t- HRRR: 4 examples provided\n527\t- NAM: 3 examples provided\n528\t- RAP: 3 examples provided\n529\t- RRFS: 3 examples provided\n530\t- NBM: 3 examples provided\n531\t- HREF: 2 examples provided\n532\t- SREF: 2 examples provided\n533\t- RTMA: 3 examples provided\n534\t\n535\t✅ **Note any variations in URL structure between different models**\n536\t- Forecast hour encoding variations documented (f00 vs 00 vs f000 vs f001)\n537\t- Cycle frequency variations documented (hourly vs 6-hourly vs 4x daily)\n538\t- Platform variations documented (AWS vs NOMADS vs FTP)\n539\t- Product type variations documented (surface vs pressure vs native)\n540\t\n541\t---\n542\t\n543\t## References\n544\t\n545\t1. Previous research: docs/research/noaa-archive-urls.md\n553\t\n554\t---\n555\t\n556\t*Document completed for bead bf-5gsm on 2026-07-03*\n557\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"32c9a23d-c5cb-4c72-96af-d868576cef03","uuid":"fe08c6a6-7746-4931-bc4c-5e39ee27f398","timestamp":"2026-07-03T12:02:05.558Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/gribtract/docs/research/bf-5gsm-noaa-url-patterns.md","content":"# NOAA Archive URL Patterns: Dates, Cycles, and Forecast Hours\n\n## Summary\n\nThis document provides a comprehensive reference for constructing NOAA GRIB2 archive URLs by explaining how dates, cycle runs, and forecast hours are encoded in the URL structure for different models.\n\n**Date:** 2026-07-03  \n**Purpose:** Reference for constructing URLs to access NOAA model data archives\n\n---\n\n## URL Pattern Components\n\n### 1. Date Encoding (YYYYMMDD)\n\nAll NOAA models use the **YYYYMMDD** format for dates in URLs:\n- **YYYY** = 4-digit year (e.g., 2026)\n- **MM** = 2-digit month (01-12)\n- **DD** = 2-digit day (01-31)\n\n**Examples:**\n- `20260703` = July 3, 2026\n- `20240601` = June 1, 2024\n- `20250115` = January 15, 2025\n\n### 2. Cycle Hour Encoding (HH, CC, or tCCz)\n\nCycle hours indicate when the model run was initiated. Encoding varies by model:\n\n| Pattern | Description | Usage |\n|---------|-------------|-------|\n| **HH** | Two-digit hour (00-23) | NAM directory naming |\n| **CC** | Two-digit hour (00-23) | RAP, RRFS file naming |\n| **tCCz** | \"t\" + two-digit hour + \"z\" | HRRR, NAM, RAP file naming |\n\n**Standard Cycle Times by Model:**\n- **HRRR:** Hourly (00z, 01z, 02z, ..., 23z)\n- **RAP:** Hourly (00z, 01z, 02z, ..., 23z)\n- **RRFS:** Hourly (00z, 01z, 02z, ..., 23z)\n- **NAM:** 6-hourly (00z, 06z, 12z, 18z)\n- **NBM:** 6-hourly (00z, 06z, 12z, 18z)\n- **HREF:** Typically (00z, 06z, 12z, 18z)\n- **SREF:** 4x daily (03z, 09z, 15z, 21z)\n\n### 3. Forecast Hour Encoding (fFF, FF, or FFF)\n\nForecast hours indicate how far into the future the prediction extends. Encoding varies:\n\n| Pattern | Description | Range | Usage |\n|---------|-------------|-------|-------|\n| **fFF** | \"f\" + two-digit hour | f00-f48 | HRRR directory naming |\n| **FF** | Two-digit hour | 00-60+ | NAM, RAP file suffix |\n| **FFF** | Three-digit hour | 001-084+ | NBM file suffix |\n\n**Forecast Hour Examples:**\n- `f00` or `00` = Analysis (initial time step)\n- `f03` or `03` = 3-hour forecast\n- `f12` or `12` = 12-hour forecast\n- `f48` or `48` = 48-hour forecast\n\n---\n\n## Model-Specific URL Patterns\n\n### 1. HRRR (High-Resolution Rapid Refresh)\n\n**URL Pattern:**\n```\nhttps://noaa-hrrr-bdp-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/hrrr.YYYYMMDD/conus/hrrr.tCCz.wrfsfcfFF.grib2\n```\n\n**Components:**\n- **YYYYMMDD** = Model run date (e.g., `20260703`)\n- **CC** = Cycle hour (00-23)\n- **FF** = Forecast hour (00-48)\n\n**Example URLs:**\n```\n# July 3, 2026, 00z cycle, analysis (f00)\nhttps://noaa-hrrr-bdp-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/hrrr.20260703/conus/hrrr.t00z.wrfsfcf00.grib2\n\n# July 3, 2026, 06z cycle, 3-hour forecast (f03)\nhttps://noaa-hrrr-bdp-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/hrrr.20260703/conus/hrrr.t06z.wrfsfcf03.grib2\n\n# July 3, 2026, 12z cycle, 12-hour forecast (f12)\nhttps://noaa-hrrr-bdp-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/hrrr.20260703/conus/hrrr.t12z.wrfsfcf12.grib2\n\n# June 1, 2024, 12z cycle, analysis (f00)\nhttps://noaa-hrrr-bdp-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/hrrr.20240601/conus/hrrr.t12z.wrfsfcf00.grib2\n```\n\n**Alternative AWS Structure (with subdirectories):**\n```\nhttps://noaa-hrrr-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/YYYYMMDD/HH/fFF/hrrr.tCCz.wrfsfcfFF.grib2\n```\n- **HH** = Cycle hour directory (00-23)\n- **fFF** = Forecast hour directory (f00-f48)\n\n**Examples with subdirectories:**\n```\nhttps://noaa-hrrr-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/20260703/00/f00/hrrr.t00z.wrfsfcf00.grib2\nhttps://noaa-hrrr-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/20260703/06/f12/hrrr.t06z.wrfsfcf12.grib2\n```\n\n**Key Variations:**\n- **Product types:** `wrfsfc` (surface), `wrfprs` (pressure), `wrfnat` (native)\n- **Sub-hourly:** `wrfsubhfFF.MM.grib2` (MM = minute offset)\n\n---\n\n### 2. NAM CONUS (North American Mesoscale)\n\n**URL Pattern:**\n```\nhttps://noaa-nam-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/nam.YYYYMMDD/nam.tCCz.[product]FF.tm00.grib2\n```\n\n**Components:**\n- **YYYYMMDD** = Model run date (e.g., `20260703`)\n- **CC** = Cycle hour (00, 06, 12, 18 only)\n- **[product]** = Product type (awphys, conusnest, etc.)\n- **FF** = Forecast hour (00-60)\n\n**Example URLs:**\n```\n# July 2, 2026, 12z cycle, analysis (00)\nhttps://noaa-nam-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/nam.20260702/nam.t12z.awphys00.tm00.grib2\n\n# July 2, 2026, 12z cycle, 12-hour forecast (12)\nhttps://noaa-nam-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/nam.20260702/nam.t12z.awphys12.tm00.grib2\n\n# January 15, 2025, 00z cycle, analysis (00)\nhttps://noaa-nam-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/nam.20250115/nam.t00z.awphys00.tm00.grib2\n```\n\n**Alternative Directory Structure:**\n```\nhttps://noaa-nam-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/YYYYMMDD_HH/\n```\n- **YYYYMMDD_HH** = Date with underscore-separated hour (e.g., `20260703_00`)\n\n**Examples:**\n```\nhttps://noaa-nam-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/20260703_00/nam.t00z.awphys00.tm00.grib2\nhttps://noaa-nam-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/20260703_12/nam.t12z.awphys12.tm00.grib2\n```\n\n**Key Variations:**\n- **Product types:** `awphys` (AWIP physics grid 218), `conusnest` (CONUS nest)\n- **Cycle limitation:** Only 4 cycles per day (00z, 06z, 12z, 18z)\n\n---\n\n### 3. RAP (Rapid Refresh)\n\n**URL Pattern:**\n```\nhttps://noaa-rap-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/YYYYMMDD/HH/rap.tCCz.awp130pgrbfFF.grib2\n```\n\n**Components:**\n- **YYYYMMDD** = Model run date (e.g., `20260703`)\n- **HH** = Cycle hour directory (00-23)\n- **CC** = Cycle hour in filename (00-23)\n- **FF** = Forecast hour (00-51)\n\n**Example URLs:**\n```\n# July 3, 2026, 00z cycle, analysis (f00)\nhttps://noaa-rap-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/20260703/00/rap.t00z.awp130pgrbf00.grib2\n\n# July 3, 2026, 12z cycle, 12-hour forecast (f12)\nhttps://noaa-rap-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/20260703/12/rap.t12z.awp130pgrbf12.grib2\n\n# July 3, 2026, 06z cycle, 21-hour forecast (f21)\nhttps://noaa-rap-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/20260703/06/rap.t06z.awp130pgrbf21.grib2\n```\n\n**Key Variations:**\n- **Grid resolutions:** `awp130pgrb` (13km), `awp252pgrb` (20km), `awp236pgrb` (40km)\n- **Extended forecasts:** 0-51h available at 03z, 09z, 15z, 21z\n- **Standard forecasts:** 0-21h for all cycles\n\n---\n\n### 4. RRFS (Rapid Refresh Forecast System)\n\n**URL Pattern:**\n```\nhttps://noaa-rrfs-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/YYYYMMDD/HH/rrfs.tCCz.[product].fFF.grib2\n```\n\n**Components:**\n- **YYYYMMDD** = Model run date (e.g., `20260703`)\n- **HH** = Cycle hour directory (00-23)\n- **CC** = Cycle hour in filename (00-23)\n- **FF** = Forecast hour (000-084+)\n\n**Example URLs:**\n```\n# July 3, 2026, 00z cycle, analysis (f000)\nhttps://noaa-rrfs-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/20260703/00/rrfs.t00z.prslev.f000.grib2\n\n# July 3, 2026, 06z cycle, 12-hour forecast (f012)\nhttps://noaa-rrfs-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/20260703/06/rrfs.t06z.prslev.f012.grib2\n\n# July 3, 2026, 12z cycle, 48-hour forecast (f048)\nhttps://noaa-rrfs-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/20260703/12/rrfs.t12z.prslev.f048.grib2\n```\n\n**Key Variations:**\n- **Product types:** `prslev` (pressure levels), `sfc` (surface), `nat` (native)\n- **Status:** BETA/Prototype (operational ~August 2026)\n- **Forecast range:** 0-18h hourly cycles; 0-84h for 00,06,12,18z cycles\n\n---\n\n### 5. NBM (National Blend of Models)\n\n**URL Pattern:**\n```\nhttps://noaa-nbm-grib2-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/blend.YYYYMMDD/HH/core/blend.tCCz.core.fFFF.co.grib2\n```\n\n**Components:**\n- **YYYYMMDD** = Model run date (e.g., `20260703`)\n- **HH** = Cycle hour directory (00, 06, 12, 18)\n- **CC** = Cycle hour in filename (00, 06, 12, 18)\n- **FFF** = Three-digit forecast hour (001-084+)\n\n**Example URLs:**\n```\n# July 3, 2026, 00z cycle, 1-hour forecast (f001)\nhttps://noaa-nbm-grib2-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/blend.20260703/00/core/blend.t00z.core.f001.co.grib2\n\n# July 3, 2026, 12z cycle, 12-hour forecast (f012)\nhttps://noaa-nbm-grib2-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/blend.20260703/12/core/blend.t12z.core.f012.co.grib2\n\n# July 3, 2026, 18z cycle, 48-hour forecast (f048)\nhttps://noaa-nbm-grib2-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/blend.20260703/18/core/blend.t18z.core.f048.co.grib2\n```\n\n**Key Variations:**\n- **Forecast hour encoding:** Always three digits (001, 002, ..., 012, ..., 084)\n- **Cycle limitation:** Only 4 cycles per day (00z, 06z, 12z, 18z)\n- **Product type:** `core` (standard blend product)\n\n---\n\n### 6. HREF (High-Resolution Ensemble Forecast)\n\n**URL Pattern (NOMADS):**\n```\nhttps://nomads.ncep.noaa.gov/pub/data/nccf/com/href/prod/href.YYYYMMDD/HH/\n```\n\n**Components:**\n- **YYYYMMDD** = Model run date (e.g., `20260703`)\n- **HH** = Cycle hour directory (00, 06, 12, 18)\n\n**Example URLs:**\n```\n# July 3, 2026, 00z cycle directory\nhttps://nomads.ncep.noaa.gov/pub/data/nccf/com/href/prod/href.20260703/00/\n\n# July 3, 2026, 12z cycle directory\nhttps://nomads.ncep.noaa.gov/pub/data/nccf/com/href/prod/href.20260703/12/\n\n# Individual ensemble member files would be in these directories\n```\n\n**Key Variations:**\n- **Access method:** NOMADS (directory browsing via HTTPS)\n- **Cycle times:** Typically (00z, 06z, 12z, 18z)\n- **File naming:** Varies by ensemble member (arw, nmm, nssl, etc.)\n\n---\n\n### 7. SREF (Short Range Ensemble Forecast)\n\n**URL Pattern (NOMADS):**\n```\nhttps://nomads.ncep.noaa.gov/pub/data/nccf/com/sref/prod/sref.YYYYMMDD/HH/\n```\n\n**Components:**\n- **YYYYMMDD** = Model run date (e.g., `20260703`)\n- **HH** = Cycle hour directory (03, 09, 15, 21)\n\n**Example URLs:**\n```\n# July 3, 2026, 03z cycle directory\nhttps://nomads.ncep.noaa.gov/pub/data/nccf/com/sref/prod/sref.20260703/03/\n\n# July 3, 2026, 15z cycle directory\nhttps://nomads.ncep.noaa.gov/pub/data/nccf/com/sref/prod/sref.20260703/15/\n```\n\n**Key Variations:**\n- **Cycle times:** Non-standard (03z, 09z, 15z, 21z - 4x daily)\n- **Status:** Proposed for termination July 2025 (may be replaced by GEFS)\n- **File naming:** Includes ensemble member identifiers (ctl, g01, g02, etc.)\n\n---\n\n### 8. RTMA (Real-Time Mesoscale Analysis)\n\n**URL Pattern:**\n```\nhttps://noaa-rtma-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/YYYYMMDD/HH/rtma.tCCz.2dvaranl.[domain].grib2\n```\n\n**Components:**\n- **YYYYMMDD** = Analysis date (e.g., `20260703`)\n- **HH** = Analysis hour directory (00-23)\n- **CC** = Analysis hour in filename (00-23)\n- **[domain]** = Domain identifier (conus, ak, hi, pr)\n\n**Example URLs:**\n```\n# July 3, 2026, 00z analysis, CONUS domain\nhttps://noaa-rtma-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/20260703/00/rtma.t00z.2dvaranl.conus.grib2\n\n# July 3, 2026, 12z analysis, CONUS domain\nhttps://noaa-rtma-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/20260703/12/rtma.t12z.2dvaranl.conus.grib2\n\n# July 3, 2026, 06z analysis, Alaska domain\nhttps://noaa-rtma-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/20260703/06/rtma.t06z.2dvaranl.ak.grib2\n```\n\n**Key Variations:**\n- **Product type:** `2dvaranl` (2D variational analysis)\n- **Domains:** `conus`, `ak` (Alaska), `hi` (Hawaii), `pr` (Puerto Rico)\n- **Update frequency:** Hourly analyses\n\n---\n\n## Platform-Specific Pattern Summary\n\n### AWS S3 Pattern (Most Common)\n```\nhttps://noaa-[model]-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/[model].YYYYMMDD/[directory]/[file]\n```\nUsed by: HRRR, NAM, RAP, RRFS, RTMA, NBM\n\n**Characteristics:**\n- **Date format:** YYYYMMDD (8 digits, no separators)\n- **Cycle encoding:** Varies (tCCz, CC, or HH directory)\n- **Forecast encoding:** Varies (fFF, FF, or FFF)\n\n### NOMADS Pattern\n```\nhttps://nomads.ncep.noaa.gov/pub/data/nccf/com/[model]/prod/[model].YYYYMMDD/HH/\n```\nUsed by: HREF, SREF, NAM, RAP, HRRR (alternative)\n\n**Characteristics:**\n- **Date format:** YYYYMMDD (8 digits, no separators)\n- **Cycle encoding:** HH directory (2-digit hour)\n- **Access method:** Directory browsing via HTTPS\n\n### NOAA FTP Pattern\n```\nftp://[server].noaa.gov/[path]/[model]/YYYYMMDD/[files]\n```\nUsed by: NDFD, WAVEWATCH3\n\n**Characteristics:**\n- **Protocol:** FTP or HTTPS\n- **Path components:** Complex multi-level path with station/format/content identifiers\n- **Date format:** Varies by product\n\n---\n\n## Forecast Hour Encoding Comparison\n\n| Model | Analysis | 1-hour | 3-hour | 12-hour | 48-hour | Range |\n|-------|----------|--------|--------|---------|---------|-------|\n| **HRRR** | f00 | f01 | f03 | f12 | f48 | 00-48 |\n| **NAM** | 00 | 01 | 03 | 12 | 48 | 00-60 |\n| **RAP** | f00 | f01 | f03 | f12 | f48 | 00-51 |\n| **RRFS** | f000 | f001 | f003 | f012 | f048 | 000-084 |\n| **NBM** | f001 | f001 | f003 | f012 | f048 | 001-084 |\n\n**Key Pattern Differences:**\n- **HRRR/NAM/RAP:** Two-digit forecast hours (00, 01, 03, 12, 48)\n- **RRFS/NBM:** Three-digit forecast hours (000, 001, 003, 012, 048)\n- **HRRR:** Uses \"f\" prefix (f00)\n- **NAM:** No prefix (00)\n- **RRFS:** Uses \"f\" prefix with three digits (f000)\n- **NBM:** Uses \"f\" prefix with three digits (f001 - analysis is f001, not f000)\n\n---\n\n## Cycle Frequency Comparison\n\n| Model | Cycles Per Day | Cycle Hours | Format in URL |\n|-------|----------------|-------------|---------------|\n| **HRRR** | 24 | 00z-23z | tCCz (e.g., t00z, t06z, t12z) |\n| **RAP** | 24 | 00z-23z | tCCz (e.g., t00z, t06z, t12z) |\n| **RRFS** | 24 | 00z-23z | tCCz (e.g., t00z, t06z, t12z) |\n| **NAM** | 4 | 00z, 06z, 12z, 18z | tCCz (e.g., t00z, t06z) |\n| **NBM** | 4 | 00z, 06z, 12z, 18z | tCCz (e.g., t00z, t06z) |\n| **HREF** | ~4 | 00z, 06z, 12z, 18z | HH directory |\n| **SREF** | 4 | 03z, 09z, 15z, 21z | HH directory |\n| **RTMA** | 24 | 00z-23z | tCCz (e.g., t00z) |\n\n**Key Pattern Differences:**\n- **Hourly models (HRRR, RAP, RRFS, RTMA):** All 24 cycles available\n- **6-hourly models (NAM, NBM, HREF):** Only 00z, 06z, 12z, 18z\n- **SREF exception:** Uses offset cycle times (03z, 09z, 15z, 21z)\n\n---\n\n## Constructing URLs: Step-by-Step Examples\n\n### Example 1: HRRR - December 25, 2025, 18z cycle, 6-hour forecast\n\n**Step 1:** Encode the date\n- Date: December 25, 2025\n- Encoded: `20251225`\n\n**Step 2:** Encode the cycle hour\n- Cycle: 18z\n- Format: `tCCz` → `t18z`\n\n**Step 3:** Encode the forecast hour\n- Forecast: 6 hours\n- Format: `fFF` → `f06`\n\n**Step 4:** Assemble the URL\n```\nhttps://noaa-hrrr-bdp-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/hrrr.20251225/conus/hrrr.t18z.wrfsfcf06.grib2\n```\n\n### Example 2: NAM - March 15, 2026, 00z cycle, analysis\n\n**Step 1:** Encode the date\n- Date: March 15, 2026\n- Encoded: `20260315`\n\n**Step 2:** Encode the cycle hour\n- Cycle: 00z\n- Format: `tCCz` → `t00z`\n\n**Step 3:** Encode the forecast hour\n- Forecast: Analysis (0 hours)\n- Format: `FF` → `00`\n\n**Step 4:** Assemble the URL\n```\nhttps://noaa-nam-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/nam.20260315/nam.t00z.awphys00.tm00.grib2\n```\n\n### Example 3: NBM - September 10, 2026, 12z cycle, 24-hour forecast\n\n**Step 1:** Encode the date\n- Date: September 10, 2026\n- Encoded: `20260910`\n\n**Step 2:** Encode the cycle hour\n- Cycle: 12z\n- Format: `tCCz` → `t12z`\n\n**Step 3:** Encode the forecast hour\n- Forecast: 24 hours\n- Format: `fFFF` → `f024` (three digits)\n\n**Step 4:** Assemble the URL\n```\nhttps://noaa-nbm-grib2-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/blend.20260910/12/core/blend.t12z.core.f024.co.grib2\n```\n\n---\n\n## Common URL Construction Errors to Avoid\n\n### Error 1: Missing Leading Zeros\n❌ `hrrr.202673/t1z.wrfsfcf3.grib2`  \n✅ `hrrr.20260703/t01z.wrfsfcf03.grib2`\n\n**Explanation:** Dates and hours must always be two digits (with leading zeros).\n\n### Error 2: Incorrect Forecast Hour Format\n❌ `blend.t12z.core.f24.co.grib2`  \n✅ `blend.t12z.core.f024.co.grib2`\n\n**Explanation:** NBM requires three-digit forecast hours (f001, f002, ..., f024).\n\n### Error 3: Wrong Product Suffix\n❌ `hrrr.t12z.wrfprsf00.grib2` (for surface data)  \n✅ `hrrr.t12z.wrfsfcf00.grib2`\n\n**Explanation:** Product suffixes matter - `wrfsfc` for surface, `wrfprs` for pressure.\n\n### Error 4: Non-Existent Cycle\n❌ `nam.20260703/nam.t03z.awphys00.tm00.grib2`  \n✅ `nam.20260703/nam.t00z.awphys00.tm00.grib2` or `nam.t06z`\n\n**Explanation:** NAM only runs 4 cycles per day (00z, 06z, 12z, 18z), not hourly.\n\n---\n\n## Quick Reference Table\n\n| Model | Date Format | Cycle Format | Forecast Format | Example |\n|-------|-------------|--------------|------------------|---------|\n| **HRRR** | YYYYMMDD | tCCz | fFF | hrrr.t12z.wrfsfcf00.grib2 |\n| **NAM** | YYYYMMDD | tCCz | FF | nam.t12z.awphys00.tm00.grib2 |\n| **RAP** | YYYYMMDD | tCCz | fFF | rap.t12z.awp130pgrbf00.grib2 |\n| **RRFS** | YYYYMMDD | tCCz | fFF | rrfs.t12z.prslev.f000.grib2 |\n| **NBM** | YYYYMMDD | tCCz | fFFF | blend.t12z.core.f001.co.grib2 |\n| **RTMA** | YYYYMMDD | tCCz | N/A (analysis) | rtma.t12z.2dvaranl.conus.grib2 |\n| **HREF** | YYYYMMDD | HH dir | Varies | href.20260703/12/ |\n| **SREF** | YYYYMMDD | HH dir | Varies | sref.20260703/09/ |\n\n---\n\n## Acceptance Criteria Verification\n\n✅ **For each model, document the URL pattern with placeholders for date, cycle, and forecast hour**\n- All 8 models documented with clear patterns\n- Placeholders (YYYYMMDD, CC, FF, etc.) clearly defined\n\n✅ **Provide at least 2-3 example URLs with specific dates/cycles/forecast hours**\n- HRRR: 4 examples provided\n- NAM: 3 examples provided\n- RAP: 3 examples provided\n- RRFS: 3 examples provided\n- NBM: 3 examples provided\n- HREF: 2 examples provided\n- SREF: 2 examples provided\n- RTMA: 3 examples provided\n\n✅ **Note any variations in URL structure between different models**\n- Forecast hour encoding variations documented (f00 vs 00 vs f000 vs f001)\n- Cycle frequency variations documented (hourly vs 6-hourly vs 4x daily)\n- Platform variations documented (AWS vs NOMADS vs FTP)\n- Product type variations documented (surface vs pressure vs native)\n\n---\n\n## References\n\n1.
